title: AI conversation transcripts can stay level-only across five common workflows
summary: Routine implementation, architecture refactor, phase-parent creation, validator remediation, and resume summaries all pass when AI-visible wording uses Level N, phase-parent, packet, file names, and task state.
evidence: Scenario A says `Level 2` and lists checklist.md without exposing internal workflow taxonomy.
Scenario B says `Level 3` and names `decision-record.md` directly.
Scenario D says `Level 3 packet missing required file: decision-record.md`.
recommendation: Keep AI-visible confirmations level-shaped and never echo internal resolver origin labels into user summaries.

title: Scenario C has an outdated Gate 3 option label
summary: The current Gate 3 contract labels phase folder as Option E, while the prompt scenario says Option D. The realistic transcript should preserve today's Gate 3 menu and use Option E for phase folders.
evidence: Project Gate 3 options define D as skip and E as phase folder.
The phase-parent transcript remains level-only when the AI asks for Option E.
recommendation: Do not change Gate 3 wording for this research thread. Update scenario docs to refer to Option E when they mean phase folder.

title: Workflow-invariance CI should be split into live-surface and public-doc tests
summary: Live output surfaces can use a strict banned-word scan immediately; public docs need a separate test with explicit allowlists for historical research and ADR text.
evidence: Iteration 12 found live templates and emitted strings are the critical public surfaces.
Existing research.md intentionally contains historical design vocabulary that should not weaken live-template enforcement.

title: Affected generated fixtures should be rewritten in the leak-fix PR
summary: Fixtures generated from changed templates should not wait for a follow-on because stale expected outputs can reintroduce banned words through snapshot churn.
evidence: The live template replacement changes expected generated output.
recommendation: Immediately update affected fixtures generated from Level 3 and phase templates; avoid broad unrelated fixture cleanup.

title: Exact leak replacements are locked
summary: Replace generated `[capability]` placeholders with `[needed behavior]`, and replace `Sub-phase manifest` with `Sub-phase list`.
evidence: `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/templates/addendum/level3-arch/spec-level3-suffix.md` and `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/templates/addendum/level3-plus-govern/spec-level3plus-suffix.md` contain frontmatter and keyword echoes.
recommendation: Use `[needed behavior]` for user-story placeholders and `Sub-phase list` for the phase-parent purpose bullet.

summary: Converted workflow-invariance risk into five explicit AI transcript dry-runs.
before: Iteration 12 audited source surfaces and identified two public-template leaks.
after: Iteration 13 proves the common AI conversation paths can stay level-only when user-visible text avoids internal workflow vocabulary.

summary: Resolved the two open testing and fixture questions.
before: Iteration 12 left CI split and fixture rewrite timing unresolved.
after: Iteration 13 chooses split CI tests and immediate rewrite of affected generated fixtures.

summary: Locked exact wording for the two known leak fixes.
before: Iteration 12 proposed example replacements for `[capability]` and `Sub-phase manifest`.
after: Iteration 13 selects `[needed behavior]` and `Sub-phase list`, with concrete template and fixture paths.
